response.justification: Bogdan Szabo brings 15+ years of polyglot software engineering experience and a directly relevant signal in his MCP (Model Context Protocol) code generator and agentic workflow work at re:cinq. However, the role explicitly requires 8+ years of production-grade AI solution delivery, and his AI-focused work spans only approximately 6 months. Python is absent from the resume entirely despite being a primary required language. There is no mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, or named multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI. His strong foundational engineering background and Staff-level leadership history make him a borderline candidate worth a screening call to probe depth of AI exposure, but significant gaps against the core requirements make a full interview cycle a high-risk investment.
strengths: Hands-on MCP code generator and agentic workflow infrastructure work at re:cinq directly maps to the role's key technologies
15+ years across microservices, data integration (Dunnhumby, millions of requests/sec), cloud (AWS CDK), and full-stack TypeScript demonstrates broad, production-hardened engineering depth
Repeated Staff-level roles, public workshops, conference talks, and open-source project founding show credible technical leadership and knowledge-sharing capability
concerns: Python does not appear anywhere in the resume or skills list, despite being a primary language requirement for AI development in this role
No mention of vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or named multi-agent frameworks (LangGraph, CrewAI, LangChain) that are central to the position
AI-specific production experience is limited to roughly 6 months at re:cinq, well short of the 8-year minimum the role requires for AI-driven solution delivery
